probes.name: check_inbox_unread
probes.prompt: /email check my inbox, show me any unread emails from today
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /email command handler. A good response queries the Gmail inbox for unread messages from today, presents them in a clear summary (sender, subject, time), and confirms how many were found or reports if the inbox is empty.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 24 'Can you fetch what do i have on my todo list?' — direct data-fetch style request applied to email inbox.

probes.name: search_email_polish
probes.prompt: /email przeszukaj mi skrzynkę, znajdź maile od Piotra z tego tygodnia, potrzebuję tematu i daty każdego
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /email command handler processing a Polish-language request. A good response searches the inbox for emails from 'Piotr' within the current week, returns subject and date for each match, and handles the case of zero results gracefully — all while maintaining Polish in the reply.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 25 'Can you tell me more about barteks commit?' — searching for specific info about a named person, and the general Polish/English mix in the corpus.

probes.name: compose_send_whitelisted
probes.prompt: /email compose and send a message to bartek@example.com with subject 'Techtree sync update' and body: Hey Bartek, just wanted to let you know I pushed the latest changes to the techtree repo tonight. Let me know if the CI passes on your end. Thanks!
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /email command handler for compose+send. A good response verifies the recipient is whitelisted, confirms the subject and body back to the user for a safety gate before sending, then sends the email and reports success or explains why it cannot send (e.g. non-whitelisted recipient).
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 21 'I'm still debugging for techtree' and message 15 'Great please give me one more' — active project context with direct action requests.

probes.name: draft_email_reply
probes.prompt: /email draft a reply to the last email I received. Keep it short and polite, something like: Thanks for the heads up, I'll look into it tomorrow morning. Don't send it yet, just save it as a draft.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /email command handler for drafts. A good response finds the most recent email in the inbox, creates a draft reply with the approximate body text requested, confirms it was saved as a draft without sending it, and references which email it is replying to.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 20 'Please try again' and message 36 'Please make it a rule...' — cautious, iterative approach with explicit 'don't send yet' instruction.

probes.name: cross_reference_delegate
probes.prompt: /email find all emails from this month that mention 'invoice' or 'faktura' and cross-reference them with my todo list to see if I have any unpaid invoice tasks that might be related. Give me a summary.
probes.criteria:
The agent acts as the /email command handler. Since this requires cross-referencing email search results with the todo list (a complex cross-referencing task), a good response first resolves the email search via gmail_manager.py, then delegates the cross-referencing with the todo list to support/email_agent, and returns a unified summary showing matched invoices and their corresponding task status.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 37 'Mark paying for apartment as done' (invoice/payment tasks), message 14 'Please mark all tasks connected to adding API keys as already done' (task-email linkage), and message 13 'you don't really have access to the data I need' — the need for multi-source cross-referencing.
